meta.title: TiDB Database Comparisons | PingCAP
meta.description: Honest, technically reviewed comparisons of TiDB against MySQL, PostgreSQL, CockroachDB, Aurora, and more. Find the right distributed SQL database for your workload.

props.headline: Choose the Right Distributed SQL Database
props.subheadline: Honest, technically reviewed comparisons of TiDB against MySQL, PostgreSQL, CockroachDB, Aurora, and more. Reviewed by engineers making architectural decisions.

props.title: TiDB vs Other Databases
props.subtitle: Detailed, workload-driven comparisons across architecture, scalability, ACID behavior, and TCO.

items.title: TiDB vs MySQL
items.description: Scale-out writes, HTAP, and distributed transactions vs. MySQL's single-primary simplicity. The definitive guide for teams hitting write limits.

items.title: TiDB vs PostgreSQL
items.description: Strong consistency, HTAP, and horizontal scale-out compared to PostgreSQL's mature ecosystem and extension model.

items.title: TiDB vs Amazon Aurora
items.description: Cloud-native distributed SQL vs. AWS's managed relational offering. Covers TCO at scale, multi-region HA, and HTAP capabilities.

items.title: TiDB vs CockroachDB
items.description: The two most-evaluated NewSQL databases. MySQL ecosystem vs. PostgreSQL wire protocol, HTAP, and open-source licensing compared.

items.title: TiDB vs SingleStore
items.description: HTAP architecture showdown: Raft-based strong consistency and open-source licensing vs. memory-first performance.

items.title: TiDB Serverless vs PlanetScale
items.description: Two MySQL-compatible serverless databases. Auto-scaling, multi-region support, AI features, and free tier economics.

props.title: Find the Right Database for Your Workload
props.subtitle: Not sure which database to shortlist? These guides cover the landscape by workload, not by vendor.

items.title: Best Database for AI Agents
items.description: Memory layers, vector search, ACID state management, and multi-agent scale. Compares TiDB, Pinecone, Redis, pgvector, and more.

items.title: Best Vector Database for RAG
items.description: Retrieval quality, metadata filtering, hybrid search, indexing options, and production readiness. Updated quarterly.

items.title: TiDB vs Traditional Databases
items.description: How distributed SQL compares to MySQL, PostgreSQL, and Oracle across scalability, ACID guarantees, and TCO at scale.

props.title: What Should You Pick?
props.subtitle: Directional guidance by workload. Detailed comparisons are linked in sections above.

tabs.label: Single-Node OLTP
bullets: TiDB adds value once scale-out enters the conversation
Lower operational overhead for predictable workloads

tabs.label: Scale-Out Writes
tabs.description: Approaching sharding limits or needing horizontal scale
bullets: Alternative: CockroachDB if already on PostgreSQL
Native distributed transactions without application sharding logic

tabs.label: OLTP + Real-Time Analytics
tabs.description: HTAP workloads requiring live insights without ETL
bullets: Alternative: SingleStore if working set fits in memory
Unified storage model for OLTP and analytics

tabs.label: AI Agents & RAG
tabs.description: SQL state management with vector search
bullets: Alternative: pgvector for moderate scale on PostgreSQL
Native support for both transactional and retrieval workloads

tabs.label: Serverless & Variable Load
bullets: Alternative: Aurora Serverless if AWS lock-in acceptable
Cost efficiency for unpredictable traffic patterns

props.title: Whitepapers & Guides
props.subtitle: Deep dives and reference documents for formal vendor evaluations.

items.title: TiDB vs MySQL Whitepaper
items.description: Side-by-side features, architecture, data consistency models, DR options, and workload versatility. Perfect for RFPs and formal evaluations.

items.title: TiDB Architecture Comparison
items.description: How TiDB's distributed SQL architecture enables scale-out writes, HTAP, and strong consistency without sacrificing compatibility.

items.title: Migration from MySQL to TiDB
items.description: Step-by-step guide for teams evaluating a migration from MySQL. Covers operational considerations and technical challenges.

items.a: Yes. TiDB is MySQL 5.7 compatible at the protocol level, making it a drop-in replacement for many MySQL workloads. Existing tools, drivers, and SQL code work without modification.

items.q: How does TiDB handle distributed transactions?
items.a: TiDB uses a two-phase commit (2PC) protocol with Raft-based consensus to ensure ACID guarantees across distributed nodes. This is one of TiDB's core strengths versus other NewSQL systems.

items.q: What is HTAP, and why does it matter?
items.a: HTAP (Hybrid Transactional/Analytical Processing) allows you to run OLTP and analytical queries on the same dataset in real-time, without ETL pipelines or separate data warehouses. TiDB achieves this through TiKV (row storage) and TiFlash (columnar storage).

items.q: How does TiDB compare to Aurora in terms of cost?
items.a: At large scale, TiDB is typically 30–50% more cost-efficient than Aurora due to open-source licensing and better resource utilization. However, small workloads may see lower absolute costs on Aurora due to managed overhead.

items.q: Do I need to shard my data in TiDB?
items.a: No. TiDB handles sharding automatically. Data is distributed across regions based on key ranges, and you write SQL as if you're using a single database.

items.q: Which database should I choose if I'm just starting out?
items.a: Start with PostgreSQL or MySQL if your workload fits a single node. As you scale writes or need HTAP, evaluate TiDB. This progression is natural and aligns with most team's growth path.

props.title: Ready to Evaluate TiDB?
props.subtitle: Start with a free TiDB Cloud cluster and run your own comparisons against your current database.
